mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns of the S&P 500 are negative in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows positive year-over-year growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and consequently S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: If, during periods where UNRATE triggers, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is actively rising in an environment of elevated consumer price inflation, it indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs, reduces economic activity, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25bps month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth is above 3%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL conditions are met, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates a healthy and expanding manufacturing sector, signaling strong supply-side activity that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth is greater than 0.2%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.2% MoM, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals a significant slowdown in the construction sector and broader economic demand, indicating future weakness in consumer spending and investment, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of periods following a 3-month consecutive decline in HOUST, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and strong growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a robust labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, thereby supporting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change is greater than 150,000 persons
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of periods where PAYEMS 3-month average change exceeds 150,000, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected increase in the Federal Funds Rate signals a more hawkish central bank stance than anticipated, leading to an immediate repricing of risk assets and higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 basis points in a single month
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS increases by 25bps or more in a month, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth is strong but industrial production is stagnating or declining, it indicates an imbalance where robust demand is not met by domestic supply, potentially leading to inflationary pressures or increased reliance on imports, which acts as a headwind for domestic equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized quarter-over-quarter growth is greater than 2% AND INDPRO month-over-month growth is less than 0.1%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where GDPC1 growth is strong and INDPRO growth is weak, the hypothesis is falsified.
